thread.body: This is Mike from Clubhouse. Use code: 2hoursmab at www.clubhouse247golf.com/freehour. Thanks for always coming out.

Having used the Trackman software at your Dartmouth location I think it's far superior. The interface is much more intuitive and having the app is a bonus to login and review your results afterwards. 

The Foresight Falcon was very finicky - several times it didn't read the shot (even though the green arrows on the ball indicated it was in position to monitor the shot). The analysis was also less precise than Trackman - which showed an image of the actual club you're using and the angle of impact and details of whether it was centred, off the toe or heel. Overall, the experience is much better with Trackman.

The spot is amazing. Ceiling height all around gives the place a luxurious feel. The 2 tv screens within the bay for club data are also very nice. The hitting area is smaller than what I've seen other places, but this makes sense and probably allows you to change it out more frequently. 3 small items to note:

- On bay 4, the club delivery video and club data probably appeared 1 in every 10 shots for me. Perhaps I did something wrong, but figured it was worth mentioning.
- On bay 4, the spotlight that's above the ball was positioned about 1ft behind the center of the hitting spot. This might have been intentional, but I'm mentioning because it could be the reason the club data wasn't consistently appearing.
- the parking lot and sidewalk outside the building were HEAVILY salted. Because I was rushing, I didn't think to remove my boots before walking to my bay. To maintain the prestige, maybe add a sign and  cubby for guests to place their boots?

All around it's a great spot and very close to where I live in Brunello. Looking forward to more midday sessions.

Hi Clubhouse,

I received 3 hours of simulator time as a gift last Christmas and was looking to use an hour of it soon to try it out but have a couple questions:

1. Is the booking per simulator or per person? I.e when I book an hour in the sim, is that 1 hour good for only me, or can I bring a guest as well?

2. How long does it typically take to play an 18 hole course on the sim? 

3. When I book using my gifted hours, is that good for any time or does booking during the more expensive time of day rate affect anything?

Thanks,

Hello! 

1. It’s per simulator, you can bring up to 4 people.
2. That one’s a bit tricky, the settings make a big difference. We usually set the gimme circle way out (around 20 feet) and two people can finish 18 holes in under 2 hours no problem. You can change the settings right before your round, or if you have the Trackman app, set it all up before you go.
3. Gift card hours work anytime. Just message us here since summer rates are on, we’ll throw in 2 extra hours. It might be a good way to test how long it takes you to play 18 without using your gift card. www.clubhouse247golf.com/info probably says it better than us.

Thanks again!
for the two extra hours go to www.clubhouse247golf.com/freehour  and use code:2hrmab
That way it will not affect your account hours.
Fantastic. I appreciate that. As far as the track man app, I assume it's recommended to download and register/set up everything prior to going?

Yes, you can play without but its definitely worthwhile to have the app. One thing people use is "ghost rounds" where you add your friends on Trackman and you can play against their previous rounds (it shows their shots etc)
you can literally walk in hit guest add two players and get a round setup in 2 minutes. The app is just good to track and extra features.

Hi Miguel, The way I calculated how much expenses were vs profit etc.

Expenses: Rent + 1500 per month in other expenses
Example 3 sims: $4000 Rent = 5500 Per month which is 66K per year.

Income: I do my calculations on an average of 35% utilization and 40$ averaging per hour. This is conservative as when i completed my analysis last fall i used 24/7 Sim businesses in California where they are taking off. They said to expect close to 50% utilization your first year. 

3 Simulators @ 8 hours per day per simulator = 24 hours at 40$ per hour = 960$ x 365 = $350,400.00
so 350K - 66K = 284K then divide that by 2 and you would bring in roughly 142K in profit.
Considering you are just incorporating now you would expect to add anywhere from 5-20K in extra expenses in your first year.

We also usually add 10% onto the cost for the electronics as we buy in bulk, test everything and set it up with you. You get much better than retail pricing regardless. We would waive that fee on your first one as you are just getting setup. We are not looking to make money from you on the setup or equipment. The quicker you pay off your investment hopefully the quicker you will want to open a new one.

I just seen that it's recommended to place ball 6-12" to the right of center. I look at a lot of my club path numbers ect, from 12" to the right am I trying to hit the ball at centre of screen or does it make a difference for club path and face angle?

I tell people to consider you are on the tee box. Aim as if you would on the course. If you always hit directly in the center of the screen when you are 6 inches off line the ball would go left,.. if you like club data If you use the program FSX Play, you can go to the settings and enable club data, FSX play also has video so you can see your swing now as well.. it will tell you in the game where to place the ball to get the most data.
Almost... If you aimed 6 inches to the right of center and were setup 6 inches to the right that should be perfectly straight on the range.
Makes sense! Thanks
I had to think about it myself... I asked the same question.
You can set it in the exact middle it will just give you primarily only ball data.
And do I need the dots on my club face or does it just make it more accurate?

The system is used for club fitting etc, so it's either 100% accurate or won't give you results. When practicing I usually put one on the club face as it gives most info. If you want to work on a 7 iron or driver for a while I might put on all 4 so I can see where it impacts the face and some extra things. Once this comes to Canada I will add these to the system. Our foresight rep is looking into it. https://www.foresightsports.com/products/focus?srsltid=AfmBOopOCUgX_Kl6b-oG3zSl8SCKh9UJuJ5E4KJI7l2Wmlfu0U5kHrS7
So at the end of the day you won't get different results with the dots you will just get more analytics of the club
